title: Microstegium biaristatum
taxonomy: Microstegium biaristatum is classified as a type of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Microstegium, which is a group of annual grasses. This plant is native to Asia and has been naturalized in many parts of the world. Microstegium biaristatum is also known as 'prickly grass' or 'Japanese stiltgrass', and its classification is further divided into the subfamily Panicoideae. Its classification as an invasive species has raised concerns about its ecological impact.

intro: Microstegium biaristatum, also known as Japanese stiltgrass, is a highly invasive annual grass native to East Asia, characterized by its slender stems and distinctive stilt-like roots. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections.
